question: What are the main components included in the Reactor Coolant System (RCS)?

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and supports key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects various subsystems, and the PZR is integrated into the RPV to help control system pressure. Each component’s functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to further detailed sections.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation, eliminating the need for pumps or external piping, and is integrated within each NuScale Power Module. The RCS is designed to safely transfer heat from the reactor core, maintain proper coolant chemistry, and ensure uniform boron concentration, supporting both normal and transient plant operations. These features collectively enhance safety and operational reliability for each module in the NuScale Power Plant.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located within the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity during both normal and emergency operations.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems in the NuScale Power Module, detailing the integration of the reactor, steam generators, pressurizer, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV). It also outlines the configuration and boundaries of the steam generator system, emphasizing the integral design of the steam generators within the RPV and the separation of primary and secondary sides.

edges.description: Section A lists specific components and operational details (e.g., PZR, CNV, RVVs, RRVs) that are part of or interact with the RCS, while Section B provides a general design basis description and explicitly names the main RCS components (RPV, PZR, RVI), thus Section A provides details that specify Section B's general description.

Section B provides a general description of the main components included in the Reactor Coolant System (RCS), while Section A specifies details about some of these components (such as the pressurizer and chemical volume control system), thereby elaborating on the general list in Section B.
